data.title: Illumia Invests in Chennai Global Capability Center to Drive Technology and Product Innovation
data.description: Illumia, previously known as Transact + CBORD, has inaugurated a new office at the International Tech Park in Chennai, India. This move signifies the company’s commitment to expanding its global product and technology operations in India.

data.title: KuCoin and Lightnet Explore Collaboration to Advance Digital Asset and Payment Infrastructure Across Southeast Asia
data.description: On April 28, 2026, in Providenciales, Turks and Caicos Islands, KuCoin, a reputable worldwide crypto platform focused on trust, revealed its consideration for a partnership with Lightnet, a fintech firm in Southeast Asia. The collaboration aims to merge KuCoin's international digital asset trading services with Lightnet's expertise in the region.

data.title: Dreame Launches More Than 20 Smart Home Products at DREAME NEXT Living Next Showcase, Highlighting Bionic Robotic Arm Platform Expansion
data.description: Today in San Francisco, Dreame Technology, a prominent global technology company, unveiled over 20 innovative smart home products and numerous pioneering technologies at the Living Next segment of DREAME NEXT. This launch includes groundbreaking features like the centerpiece of the showcase, marking a significant milestone in the industry.

data.title: América Móvil Files Annual Report on Form 20-F
data.description: In Mexico City, América Móvil, S.A.B. de C.V. ("AMX") [BMV: AMX] [NYSE: AMX], has submitted its annual report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2025 to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"), the Mexican National Banking and Securities Commission ("CNBV"), and the Mexican Stock Exchange (Bolsa Mexicana de Valores, S.A.B. de C.V., "BMV"). The English version of the report is available on the SEC's website.

data.title: Pharos blockchain now supports USDC and Circle's CCTP for cross-chain transfers, powering DeFi with institutional-grade features.
data.description: Pharos blockchain has recently announced its support for USDC and Circle's Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol (CCTP). This integration allows for cross-chain transfers and facilitates the development of institutional-grade decentralized finance (DeFi) applications. This move is set to enhance interoperability in the crypto space, providing opportunities for seamless transactions and improved functionality. With USDC and CCTP now backed by Pharos blockchain, users can expect a more robust and versatile ecosystem.

data.title: Japan imposes stricter AML rules for crypto in real estate transactions due to money laundering risks from global transfers.
data.description: In Japan, authorities are requiring more rigorous anti-money laundering (AML) checks for cryptocurrency involvement in property transactions. They point to concerns about money laundering activities associated with cross-border transfers. This move aims to enhance scrutiny and monitoring processes to mitigate risks. The measures come amid a backdrop of escalating global financial crimes, signaling a shift toward stronger regulatory oversight in this sector. By enforcing stricter protocols, Japan is intensifying efforts to combat illicit financial activities linked to cryptocurrencies in real estate dealings.

data.title: Crypto firms face $1.06B in AML fines, outpacing SEC crackdowns in H1 2025, according to CertiK.
data.description: Fines for Anti-Money Laundering (AML) violations skyrocketed to $1.06 billion in the first half of 2025, surpassing actions taken by the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) as the primary regulatory concern for cryptocurrency companies, according to a report by CertiK.
